converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Peter Paul Kellogg papers
Kellogg, Peter Paul, 1899-1975.
21-18-893
13 cubic feet. 13 cubic feet.
1934-1964. Correspondence, manuscripts, photographs, audio recordings, clippings. Correspondence, reports, manuscripts of articles, photographs, clippings, notes, memoranda, and printed material relating to Kellogg's career in the Laboratory of Ornithology at Cornell University; the history of the Laboratory; the Albert R. Brand Bird Song Foundation; ornithological expeditions to central Africa, Manitoba, and elsewhere; and bird clubs. Collection material in English 2073551
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Mary Warren
Date completed:
February 1990
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez, January 2002
Date Modified:
RMC Staff, November 2015
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE:
Professor of ornithology and biological acoustics, Cornell University. Peter Paul Kellogg was appointed assistant professor of ornithology at Cornell University in 1938 and professor of ornithology and biological acoustics in 1953.He studied bird sounds and the vocal apparatus of birds, and pioneered the recording and study of songs of wild birds.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION:
Correspondence, reports, manuscripts of articles, photographs, clippings, notes, memoranda, and printed material relating to Kellogg's career in the Laboratory of Ornithology at Cornell University; the history of the Laboratory; the Albert R. Brand Bird Song Foundation; ornithological expeditions to central Africa, Manitoba, and elsewhere; and bird clubs. Much of the correspondence is with other ornithologists, education and conservation groups, departments, institutions, record companies, film producers, and manufacturers of technical equipment concerning the procedures and problems of taping bird calls; correspondents include Arthur A. Allen, Albert R. Brand, Myles E. W. North, Paul Schwartz, Lyman K. Stuart, Gustav A. Swanson, and various members of the Laboratory. Also, 245 phonograph records of bird songs, some with handbooks; five recordings made at Professor Allen's retirement party (1950); and handwritten notes (22 pages, 1961) made by Professor Kellogg's wife, Byrl Jorgenson Kellogg, keying recordings in the Cornell Library of Natural Sounds to references made by Louis Agassiz Fuertes in six papers on tropical bird songs published (1913-1914) in BIRD LORE.
Cite As:
Peter Paul Kellogg papers, #21-18-893. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Ornithologists. College teachers. Ornithology. Birdsongs -- Recording and reproducing. Bird lore. Allen, Arthur A. (Arthur Augustus), 1885-1964. Brand, Albert R. Kellogg, Byrl Jorgenson. North, Myles E. W. Schwartz, Paul. Stuart, Lyman K. Swanson, Gustav Adolph, 1910- Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University. Laboratory of Ornithology

Scope and Contents
Includes set of correspondence from Kellogg to Tall from Tall's daughter. Many sent from University of Miami, some form the Laboratory of Ornithology, mostly relating to audio and acoustics.Tall, Joel - Recording (Kellogg)1950-62812Taxes and Permits - Recording1951-52813Tead, D.B. - Recording1952-5381413th International Ornithology Congress1961-62815U - Departmental1952-61816University memo (mimeo)1960817University notices1958-59818V - Departmental1958-64819Voco. Kellogg, typed carbon copy with notes, ca. 1936. Handwritten notes by Byrl J. (Mrs. P.P.) Kellogg re L.A. Fuertes' articles on tropical birds in Bird Lore, ca. 1936.1913-14.23 pages10Early recordings, probably by Brand8 records111Early recordings, probably by Brand6 records112Early recordings, probably by Brand3 records113Early recordings, probably by Brand3 records114Early recordings, probably by Brand3 records115Early recordings, probably by Brand2 records116"The Songs of Wild Birds," Brand4 records117"More Songs of Wild Birds," Brand2 records118(Record of six species privately published by Cornell Laboratory)1 record119"Native Bird Songs," Victor 25765, ca. 1935; the first commercially published record from the Cornell Laboratory; thousands were sold but it was very hard to interest any publishers, so no royalties were given; a very rare recordca. 19351 record1110"American Bird Songs," vol. 1 (black) (Cornell)6 records1111"American Bird Songs," vol. 1 (red) (Cornell)6 records1112"Songbirds of America" (Cornell)1 record1113"Cornell Guide to Bird Songs," matching "A Field Guide to the Birds" by Roger Tory Peterson1 record1114"Songbirds of America in Color, Sound and Story" (Cornell)1 record & text1115"Dawn in a Duck Blind" (Cornell)1 record1116"Florida Bird Songs" (Cornell)1 record1117"Western Bird Songs" (Cornell)1 record1118"The Mockingbird Sings" (Cornell)1 record1119"Music and Bird Songs" (Cornell)1 record121Test pressings9 records122"Bird Songs East of the Rockies," Jerry and Norma Stillwell1 record123"The Whippoorwill Sings," Jerry and Norma Stillwell1 record124"The Mockingbird Sings," Jerry and Norma Stillwell1 record125Hans Traber - "So Singen Unsere Vögel"11 records1211"Vogelring Schmallplatten"1 record1212Pierre Strinati1 record1213From Dr. Stranborn -slightly chipped (1 missing)4 records1214"Songs of the Greenville Mockingbird"1 record1215"Song of the Lyre Bird"(very rare) chipped 1 record1216"Karl Reich Avery; Scope and Contents
Includes set of correspondence from Kellogg to Tall from Tall's daughter. Many sent from University of Miami, some form the Laboratory of Ornithology, mostly relating to audio and acoustics.Tall, Joel - Recording (Kellogg)1950-62812Taxes and Permits - Recording1951-52813Tead, D.B. - Recording1952-5381413th International Ornithology Congress1961-62815U - Departmental1952-61816University memo (mimeo)1960817University notices1958-59818V - Departmental1958-64819Voco. Fuertes' articles on tropical birds in Bird Lore, ca. 1936.1913-14.23 pages10Early recordings, probably by Brand8 records111Early recordings, probably by Brand6 records112Early recordings, probably by Brand3 records113Early recordings, probably by Brand3 records114Early recordings, probably by Brand3 records115Early recordings, probably by Brand2 records116"The Songs of Wild Birds," Brand4 records117"More Songs of Wild Birds," Brand2 records118(Record of six species privately published by Cornell Laboratory)1 record119"Native Bird Songs," Victor 25765, ca. 1935;
